title: Quick and Creamy Tomato Soup Ready in Just 30 Minutes
promptIMG: Food photography: creamy tomato soup, appetizing presentation, natural lighting, kitchen setting, documentary style, realistic colors --ar 4:3 --quality 2
description: This creamy tomato soup is a cozy hug in a bowl. Made with fresh tomatoes and a splash of cream, it's perfect for chilly evenings or a quick lunch!
instructions: In a large pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the diced onion and sauté for about 5 minutes until soft.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and vegetable broth. Add the dried basil, sugar, salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Let it cook for about 10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Remove from heat and use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth. If you don’t have one, carefully transfer to a blender in batches.
Stir in the heavy cream and return to low heat to warm through. Adjust seasoning if necessary.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh basil leaves.
tips: When making this soup, don't skip the sugar; it really helps balance the acidity of the tomatoes. A common mistake is not blending the soup enough—smooth is the way to go for that creamy texture! Leftovers can be stored in the fridge for up to 3 days, and they reheat beautifully on the stove. If you want a lighter version, you can substitute the heavy cream with coconut milk for a dairy-free option.

structured_content.content: There's something incredibly comforting about a warm bowl of creamy tomato soup, especially on a chilly day. This recipe is not only quick but also bursts with flavor. I love making it for lunch or as a light dinner paired with a grilled cheese sandwich. Trust me, once you try this, you'll want to keep it in your regular rotation!

For this soup, you'll want to use quality crushed tomatoes since they're the star of the dish. Fresh herbs like basil add a lovely aroma, but dried works just fine if that's what you have on hand. You can even toss in some red pepper flakes if you like a kick!

Making this soup is a breeze! You'll start by sautéing the onion and garlic until they're fragrant, then add in the tomatoes and broth. After letting it simmer for a bit, just blend it up until it's silky smooth. The addition of cream at the end takes it to the next level!

upload.context: Creamy tomato soup in a rustic bowl on a wooden table

structured_content.content: This soup is fantastic on its own, but you can also serve it with a side of crusty bread or a simple salad for a complete meal. Feel free to experiment with toppings too—croutons or a sprinkle of cheese can add a delightful crunch!
